text: The system shall allow a user to create an account using name, mobile number, email address, password, password confirmation, and acceptance of terms.
The system shall not require a government-issued ID for the user-registration path shown in the supplied user workflow; worker identity information for administrator review is governed by FR-07.
The system shall validate required registration details, matching passwords, acceptance of terms, and a valid OTP before activating an account.
The system shall give clear feedback for incomplete details, invalid OTPs, failed OTP delivery, network errors, and rejected identity information.
The system shall allow a service provider to create a worker account.
The system shall allow a worker to select one or more service categories.
The system shall allow a worker to submit identity information for administrator review.
The system shall allow a worker to define availability and record relevant work experience.
The system shall require administrator approval before a worker can accept jobs.
The system shall allow a user to create a service request with service choice, issue description, optional photos, address, schedule, budget, and notes.

The system shall evaluate workers using service category and skills, availability and preferred schedule, subdivision or service-area location and distance, ratings and reviews, and active recommendation-subscription priority without overriding suitability.
The system shall present eligible workers and discovery results to the user.
The system shall use an active worker recommendation subscription for priority placement without overriding suitability or availability.
The system shall allow a user to select a worker and send a booking request based on a service request.
The system shall make request details available only to the selected or matching worker and authorized administrators.
The system shall allow an approved worker to view an incoming request and accept or decline it.
The system shall support the statuses Pending, Accepted, Worker Preparing, Worker En Route, Worker Arrived, Service Started, In Progress, Completed, and Cancelled.
The system shall allow authorized participants to monitor status changes, cancel a booking, give a cancellation reason, view the refund policy, and confirm cancellation.
The system shall provide an administrator dashboard after successful administrator sign-in.
The system shall allow administrators to review worker information, approve workers, or request more documents.
The system shall allow administrators to manage user and worker accounts, profiles, categories, availability, and approval status.

The system shall allow administrators to view transactions and payment details, including cash-confirmation records and configured cash commission or homeowner-charge values, and decide whether a refund is processed or rejected.
The system shall allow administrators to manage reports, analytics, exports, print outputs, and platform records.

The system shall allow administrators to moderate submitted homeowner ratings and reviews before publication, manage support tickets, escalate unresolved issues, handle booking problems, and record interventions.
The system shall support Cash as the payment method for a completed booking. Cash shall follow the confirmation flow in FR-26.

The system shall process Cash payment and record whether payment was successful. It shall require homeowner cash-payment confirmation and worker payment-received confirmation before marking payment successful.

The system shall generate a receipt after successful Cash payment and close the booking. The receipt shall include the service amount, commission rate and amount, worker net amount, and homeowner platform charge.
The system shall show a Cash payment failure and let the user retry Cash payment.
The system shall allow a user or homeowner to give a star rating after a completed and paid booking.
The system shall allow a user or homeowner to submit a written review and upload review images after a completed and paid booking.
The system shall display administrator-moderated worker ratings and reviews in worker details and profiles.
The system shall prevent ratings and reviews for bookings that are not completed and paid.
The system shall allow a user to upload photos for a service request.
The system shall allow a user to add a written description to a service request.
The system shall make request photos and descriptions available only to authorized matching workers and administrators.
The system shall provide clear feedback when photo capture or upload fails.
The system shall request location permission before accessing device location.
The system shall use available location information to support nearby-worker discovery and live booking tracking.
The system shall record a chosen service address for a booking.
The system shall explain denied or unavailable location access and let the user retry after enabling location.
The system shall provide map tracking and an ETA when location permission is granted during a worker-en-route booking.
The system shall support recording a worker starting location when relevant.
The system shall make booking location context available only to authorized booking participants.
The system may provide directions or travel-distance information when supported by the selected location implementation.
The system shall support message translation between Filipino and English.
The system shall preserve the original message when displaying a translation result.
The system shall inform users when a requested translation is outside the Filipino-English MVP scope.
The system shall provide clear feedback when message translation fails.
The system shall sign the user in automatically after successful account creation.
The system shall provide a worker onboarding screen when the worker is not authenticated.
The system shall show the worker whether they are approved or need to provide more documents.
The system shall let a user browse featured, recommended, and recent workers.
The system shall let a user search, filter, sort, and select a service category when finding workers.
The system shall show a worker’s ratings, reviews, skills, experience, availability, portfolio, and pricing in worker details.
The system shall show the top five matching workers and let a user view and compare workers.
The system shall let a user message a worker before selecting that worker.

The system shall show that no workers are available and let a user adjust filters or date when no suitable match is found; for an AI-created request, it shall also keep the request open and notify the user later as specified by FR-98.
The system shall let a worker provide a reason when declining a booking request.
The system shall handle worker offline, decline, and booking-timeout outcomes and recommend another worker.
The system shall let a user choose a service, describe the issue, upload photos, choose an address and schedule, set a budget, add notes, and request AI analysis as shown in the workflow.
The system shall provide Call, Chat, and Emergency contact options during an active booking.
The system shall organize bookings into Upcoming, Ongoing, Completed, and Cancelled views and shall provide booking details.
The system shall show an error when administrator credentials are invalid.
The system shall let an administrator manage their profile, password, email, two-factor authentication, login history, and logout.
The system shall provide audit logs, a trash area, restore actions, and permanent deletion after an administrator decides not to restore an item.
The system shall let an administrator change system settings, save changes, or discard unsaved changes.
The system shall let an administrator create notifications for users, workers, or everyone and send or schedule them.
The system shall let an administrator generate reports, view analytics dashboards, and export or print report results.
The system shall let an administrator view support tickets and ticket details, close resolved tickets, and escalate unresolved tickets.
The system shall let an administrator suspend a user after reviewing user details.
The system shall let an administrator review booking details, identify a booking problem, intervene, mark it resolved, and manage services when no problem is identified.
The administrator interface shall provide search, filters, pagination, export, notifications, confirmations, and success or error feedback.

The system shall record payment details, payment status, the related booking reference, required cash confirmations, configured cash commission (default 10%), worker net amount, and homeowner platform charge (default PHP 0).
The system shall record an administrator’s decision to process or reject a refund.
The system shall let a user recommend a worker as part of sending feedback.
The system shall show a feedback-submitted result after a review is sent.
The system shall let a user save and manage service addresses.
The system shall provide a chat list and worker chat with image sharing, location sharing, and optional voice messages.
The system shall provide alerts for updates, worker messages, payment updates, promotions, and system notifications.

The system shall provide profile functions for personal information, notification preferences, saved addresses, settings, payment methods, privacy, booking history, favourites, help center, and logout.
The system shall provide user navigation for Browse Categories, Send Request, Bookings, Messages, Alerts, and Profile.
The system shall provide worker navigation for Dashboard, Job Posts (the canonical name for the legacy Browse view), Bookings, Reviews, and Profile.
The system shall let a worker browse job posts, view request details, and accept a job.
The system shall let a worker start and complete an accepted job.
The system shall let a worker contact the customer through messaging and use live tracking when available.
The system shall show a worker’s bookings, payment-received status, review history, and provider details.
The system shall provide worker profile settings.

The system shall provide a post-payment Rate & Review modal that displays the customer?s submitted rating and review for the related completed, paid booking. The worker shall view this feedback only and shall not submit a rating or review through the modal. Customer feedback remains subject to administrator moderation before publication.
The system shall let a registered user sign in with email address or phone number and password, and choose Remember Me.
The system shall let a user reset a forgotten password by sending a reset link or OTP and setting a new password.
The system shall show an incorrect-credentials error and let the user try signing in again.
The system shall provide an optional AI Home Assistant that a user can open when preparing a service request.
The system shall let a user submit an issue photo or a spoken issue description for AI analysis, and shall convert supported spoken input to text.
The system shall analyze the submitted issue and return a detected issue type and severity.
The system shall show the AI’s possible cause, suggested service category, estimated cost range, and safety advice.
The system shall create an editable service-request draft from available AI analysis details; the user shall review or change the draft before sending the booking request.
The system shall let a user save an AI analysis and service-request draft to continue later.
The system shall keep an AI-created request open when no suitable worker is found and notify the user when a suitable match is found later.
The system shall show a Splash Screen after the user launches the mobile application and shall then show the Landing Page.
The Landing Page shall present an Already Registered? decision that routes a registered visitor to Sign In and a new visitor to Create Account.
After successful account creation or sign-in, the system shall open the Home screen, which provides access to the user navigation required by FR-81.
The system shall treat the worker-workflow Switch to User control as a temporary prototype-testing control and shall not provide worker-to-user role switching as a production feature.

The system shall treat Accept Worker, Hire, Worker Match, and Booking Success nodes shown in the worker prototype as user-side matching and booking actions. The production worker workspace shall use Job Posts, request details, accept, start, complete, bookings, reviews, and profile as defined by FR-82 through FR-87.

The system shall use the canonical booking lifecycle in FR-17: Pending, Accepted, Worker Preparing, Worker En Route, Worker Arrived, Service Started, In Progress, Completed, and Cancelled. Simplified workflow diagrams are summary views and do not replace these statuses.
The system shall provide responsive feedback for account, discovery, AI analysis, booking, location, payment, message, notification, and administration actions.
The system shall indicate loading or processing status when a result is not immediately available.
The system shall return a clear outcome when an OTP, location, AI-analysis, payment, notification, translation, or external-service action cannot be completed.
The system shall retain confirmed account, AI analysis and draft, booking, payment, receipt, review, notification, support, and audit records according to the selected data-storage design.
The system shall not represent incomplete submissions as successful completed actions.
The system shall handle unavailable camera, microphone, location, network, AI-analysis, payment, translation, and external service capabilities without causing the interface to fail unexpectedly.
The system shall enforce role-appropriate access for users, workers, and administrators.
The system shall protect identity, account-security, and verification information from unauthorized access.
The system shall protect AI analyses and drafts, booking, payment, review, message, notification, and location information from unauthorized access.
The system shall request consent before accessing camera, photo library, microphone, or location data where required.
The system shall collect and present only information needed for the workflow-aligned functions.
The system shall use clear, consistent labels and status indicators.
The system shall provide understandable validation and error messages.
The system shall make Filipino-English translation available without replacing the original message.
The system shall explain the effect of denying camera, microphone, or location permission on the relevant feature.
The system shall keep account, AI analysis, matching, booking, payment, communication, and administration responsibilities sufficiently separated for future enhancement.
The system shall allow post-MVP capabilities to be evaluated without redefining the workflow-aligned MVP scope.
The system shall retain traceable requirement identifiers for implementation and testing.
